story.description: A clip of connected habitats, biodiversity, food webs, or ecosystem interactions: wild iberian lynx in natural habitat
story.hook: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders.
story.lead: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders. Watch the reef texture, because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat. Fish, algae, and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city. The color sits on top of engineering. Which habitat next?
story.script: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders. Watch the reef texture, because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat. Fish, algae, and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city. The color sits on top of engineering. Which habitat next?

story.description: A clip of microscope footage, cells, microbes, algae, crystals, or tiny biology: glowing green microorganisms in motion
story.hook: Cells have to copy themselves before dividing.
story.lead: Cells have to copy themselves before dividing. Watch the cell boundary, because division only works after the internal instructions are copied and organized. Then the cell can split the material into two working lives. At this scale, reproduction is careful logistics. Which tiny process next?
story.script: Cells have to copy themselves before dividing. Watch the cell boundary, because division only works after the internal instructions are copied and organized. story.description: A clip of microscope footage, cells, microbes, algae, crystals, or tiny biology: vertical stop motion video of cutouts of microorganism
story.hook: Cells have to copy themselves before dividing.
story.lead: Cells have to copy themselves before dividing. Watch the cell boundary, because division only works after the internal instructions are copied and organized. Then the cell can split the material into two working lives. At this scale, reproduction is careful logistics. Which tiny process next?
story.script: Cells have to copy themselves before dividing. Watch the cell boundary, because division only works after the internal instructions are copied and organized. story.description: A clip of primates: monkeys observing nature on stone pathway
story.hook: Monkeys can change habits to solve small problems
story.lead: Monkeys pause on stone paths and calculate. That tilted head isn't just cute—it's active problem-solving. Their eyes lock onto details we miss because they're literally working the puzzle in front of them. That's why they hold still mid-step. They're deciding the next move.
story.script: Monkeys pause on stone paths and calculate. That tilted head isn't just cute—it's active problem-solving. Their eyes lock onto details we miss because they're literally working the puzzle in front of them. That's why they hold still mid-step. They're deciding the next move.
